div.p: To all Christ's faithful who shall see or hear this written deed, Thomas de Aunay, knight, wishes eternal salvation in the Lord. Let your whole community know me to have given, granted and by this my present charter to have made firm to God and to Blessed Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place now and to come, for ever, in free and everlasting almsthe lands of ‘Brunsceth’, Auchencrieff, and Dargavel, by their correct boundaries, with all their pertinents and whatever is comprised within the same bounds as well in men as in pastures, buildings, meadows, moors, marshes, waters, mills, woods and all other things, so that they hold and have all the foregoing things with their pertinents so freely in perpetuity as I held them, and in all the foregoing things I have withheld nothing to myself. Indeed, I and my successors shall warrant all the foregoing to the same monks for ever. In testimony of this matter I have applied my seal to the present written deed. With these witnesses: John of Maxwell; Thomas son of Ranulf, sheriff of Dumfries; John of Mandeville, Walter Crook, and Thomas Maitland, knights; Laurence of Brackley; Thomas of Kent; and many others. Given in the year of the Lord 1237.
